title: asperula exasperata subsp. tephrocarpa
intro: Asperula exasperata subsp. tephrocarpa is a subspecies of the Asperula exasperata plant, a type of flowering plant in the mint family (Lamiaceae). It is a perennial herb that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Asperula exasperata subsp. tephrocarpa include its ability to treat wounds, reduce inflammation, and prevent infections, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in herbal preparations to support digestive health and immune function.
